depth_guidance.deep: Capture weaker but still meaningful secondary findings as well.
depth_guidance.standard: Identify the main findings across the theme inventory.
depth_guidance.surface: Identify only the clearest and best-supported findings.

analytical_dimensions.description: Identify the discrete AOI findings that deserve naming.
probing_questions: What exact problem is visible here?

depth_guidance.deep: Include denser evidence and clearer consequence analysis.
depth_guidance.standard: Include document labels, locators, and short excerpts.

analytical_dimensions.description: Preserve concrete target/source provenance for each finding.
probing_questions: Which target passage demonstrates the problem?
Which source passage contradicts or complicates it?

depth_scaling.deep: Classify primary and secondary findings with richer rationale.
depth_scaling.standard: Classify all main findings across themes.

capabilities.description: Assign exactly one dominant AOI sin type per finding.

depth_scaling.standard: Explicit document labels, locators, and excerpts.

capabilities.description: Keep each finding anchored to target-side and source-side evidence.

shares_with.findings: Flat AOI findings keyed by theme and sin type.

depth_levels.description: Only the most obvious AOI findings with short evidence anchors.
depth_levels.suitable_for: Fast triage of the strongest AOI failures.

depth_levels.description: Flat AOI findings with provenance, severity, and implication.
depth_levels.suitable_for: Default AOI finding extraction for the single-thinker tranche.

depth_levels.description: More exhaustive AOI findings with denser evidence and stronger nuance.
depth_levels.suitable_for: Richer AOI review when the corpus supports more findings.

key_concepts.definition: A bounded category of problematic reuse, omission, distortion, or authority borrowing.
key_concepts.name: scholarly_sin

key_concepts.definition: Pairing target-side evidence with source-side evidence or explicit silence.
key_concepts.name: provenance_pairing

primary.description: Bloom's anxiety-of-influence vocabulary frames the recognizable misuses and agonistic distortions at stake.
primary.name: bloom

secondary.description: Citation practice and scholarly apparatus matter because AOI failures often appear as omissions, selective use, or authority laundering.
secondary.name: grafton

traditions.description: AOI findings rely on disciplined source/target provenance rather than impressionistic accusation.
traditions.name: citation_scholarship

findings.sin_type: misreading | unacknowledged_debt | misappropriation | decontextualization | selective_citation | flattening | ventriloquism | strategic_silence | premature_synthesis | legitimation_borrowing
findings.source_document_id: string - stable slug for the source work
findings.source_quote: string or null for strategic silence
findings.theme_id: string - must match an upstream theme identifier
findings.title: string

problematique:
Anxiety of Influence becomes concrete at the level of specific findings: named
scholarly sins tied to a theme, a target passage, a source passage, and an
account of what the subject author misses. This engine identifies those flat,
evidence-bearing findings without yet turning them into a reading surface.

The emphasis is discriminating classification, provenance, and argument-level
consequence. Each finding must be classifiable into exactly one AOI sin type,
tied back to the theme inventory, and grounded in target/source textual
evidence or strategic silence where the absence of engagement is itself the
scholarly problem.
researcher_question: What specific AOI findings emerge when the subject author's engagement with the selected source thinker is tested theme by theme?
